title: Tau Thioflavin T Assay
author: Alexandra Netter-Glangeaud
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.wxgffjw
materials: Molecular Devices Gemini XPS Microplate Reader
10 uM Heparin was added to each well.

steps.title: A 1mM stock solution of Thioflavin...
steps.instruction: A 1mM stock solution of Thioflavin T was prepared in dH2O (prepared fresh and filtered through a 0.2 µm syringe filter).

steps.title: The thioflavin T was diluted in PBS pH 7
steps.instruction: The thioflavin T was diluted in PBS pH 7.4 so that the final Thioflavin T concentration in each well was 25 µM (volume per well = 100 µL).

steps.title: Tau aliquots were thawed at room temperature just before use
steps.instruction: Tau aliquots were thawed at room temperature just before use.

steps.title: Either fibril or monomer (or both)...
steps.instruction: Either fibril or monomer (or both) was added to the appropriate wells. The well contents were pipetted up and down to mix.

steps.title: The plate was sealed and placed...
steps.instruction: The plate was sealed and placed in a shaking incubator (800 rpm) at 37 °C

steps.title: Fluorescence was measured on a Molecular...
steps.instruction: Fluorescence was measured on a Molecular Devices Gemini XPS Microplate Reader using Softmax Pro software version 6.5.1. XPS Microplate Reader Settings: Temperature: 37°C Read Type: Well Scan Wavelength: Excitation at 450 nm and Emission at 485 nm PMT Gain: Automatic Flashes per read: 6 Shake: 20 seconds before read

steps.title: The plate was re-sealed and placed...
steps.instruction: The plate was re-sealed and placed into the shaking incubator at 37°C.

steps.title: Readings were taking at regular intervals...
steps.instruction: Readings were taking at regular intervals from 1 hour to 72 hours.
